meta.title: MUD - To Help Secure IoT
meta.description:
Marc Blackmer of Cisco introduces the Manufacturer Usage Description (MUD) at S4x17. MUD is an extension to DHCP that includes a URI to the IoT manufacturer. The device then uses this URI to download configuration information that defines what the device is suppose to do. 

Marc uses the phrase "keeping devices into their own swim lanes" a few times in the video, and this is a good analogy with the goal of preventing lateral movement.

In the Mirai example, why would by IP camera be trying to go to this website? This does not fit the MUD provided configuration profile.

MUD is an IETF Internet Draft available at https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-lear-ietf-netmod-mud-04
